summary: Up from 3.9 to 4.0 — first time hitting 4. Major wins this round: constants fully extracted to utils/ (DEFAULT_ROSTER, DESKS, VIBE_WHITEBOARD all in constants.js, VIBE_QUOTES in quotes.js — FINALLY after 8 flags), React.memo applied to both CharacterAvatar and Character, WindowElement now uses local time correctly, date display no longer hardcoded to America/Los_Angeles, info button tabIndex properly gated by isFlipped, and aria-label added to vibeStreak span. Six long-standing issues resolved. What remains: whiteboard word-break at 375px, vibeStreak sessionStorage mechanic still broken (increments on effect re-run, not on sync), and a few polish items. The codebase is noticeably cleaner.

suggestions.title: Whiteboard text breaks mid-word at 375px viewport — 5th flag
suggestions.description: Office.css: .office-whiteboard is 5.5% wide with font-size 0.42rem and word-break: break-word. At 375px that's ~20px wide. '🏆 SHIP IT' renders as broken fragments. Fix: at the existing 480px media query breakpoint, either hide the text and show only the emoji via a ::before pseudo-element, or bump width to 7.5%. The text is decorative, not critical — hiding it on small screens is perfectly fine.

suggestions.title: vibeStreak counter increments on effect re-run, not on actual sync cycles
suggestions.description: App.jsx: The vibeStreak useEffect runs on [teamVibe.key] changes. But sessionStorage persists within a tab, so refreshing the page increments the streak even if no sync happened. A user refreshing 10 times gets streak x10. Fix: move the streak logic into syncFromGitHub callback so it only increments on actual successful poll responses. Store the previous vibe key in a ref instead of sessionStorage.

suggestions.title: Confetti layer renders 30 DOM elements on every vibe upgrade
suggestions.description: App.jsx: The confetti burst creates 30 <span> elements. These are inserted into the DOM on vibe upgrade and removed after 2.5s. Consider using CSS @keyframes with animation-fill-mode: forwards and removing the elements after animation completes, or use a canvas-based approach for smoother rendering. Current approach works but creates layout thrash.

suggestions.title: Office floor view has no keyboard navigation for characters
suggestions.description: Office.jsx: Characters have tabIndex={0} but no visible focus indicator in CSS. The .char class has pointer-events: none by default (overridden for some variants). Add a :focus-visible outline to .char and ensure all interactive characters (manager, ghost, working, idle) have pointer-events: auto consistently.

suggestions.title: Ghost OOO bubble has no dismiss mechanism on mobile
suggestions.description: Office.jsx: Ghost characters show an OOO bubble on hover (onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave). On touch devices, there's no hover event. The bubble only shows on focus, which requires tapping. But there's no visual affordance indicating these are interactive. Add a subtle tap indicator or use a toggle pattern (tap to show, tap again to dismiss).

suggestions.title: Ambient speech bubble interval (45s) creates subtle memory leak pattern
suggestions.description: Office.jsx Character: The ambient interval fires every 45s and sets a 4s timeout. If the component unmounts between the interval firing and the timeout resolving, the timeout still runs (setBubble on unmounted component). The cleanup only clears the interval and timerRef, but timerRef is only set for hover timeouts, not ambient ones. Store the ambient timeout ID and clear it on unmount.

suggestions.title: Solo spotlight message could be more dynamic
suggestions.description: App.jsx: When only one worker is active, a static message 'Running solo today...' is shown. Consider rotating through 3-4 messages based on the worker's role or how long they've been solo. Small delight that makes repeated visits more engaging.

suggestions.title: Add worker uptime/session duration to activity log entries
suggestions.description: Currently activity log entries only show timestamp and message. For 'complete' type entries, it would be useful to show how long the worker was active (e.g., 'Completed in 45m'). This data could be computed from hire→complete timestamp pairs in the activity log.
